headline: 67 addresses catalogued across Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon, Base, Solana, and TRON; 8 governance/admin/multisig contracts, 4 core protocol contracts, 55+ merchants; Solana and TRON deployments surfaced but on-chain addresses not fetchable this run.

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search confirmed WBTC token at 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 (Ethereum). Arbiscan confirmed WBTC bridged token at 0x2f2a2543B76A4166549F7aaB2e75Bef0aefC5B0f with proxy implementation at truncated 0x3f770Ac6...21265aD46 (Arbitrum). Optimism etherscan confirmed 0x68f180fcCE6836688E9084f035309E29Bf0A2095. PolygonScan confirmed 0x1bfd67037b42cf73acf2047067bd4f2c47d9bfd6 (UChildERC20 proxy) with implementation 0x7ffb3d637014488b63fb9858e279385685afc1e2. BaseScan confirmed WBTCOFT at 0x0555E30da8f98308EdB960aa94C0Db47230d2B9c. BSC pseudo-WBTC at 0x8b9b4c5bFc50Bab521bF8016054fC8afbc381400 appears to be a scam/unofficial token (different decimals/supply), not catalogued.

wbtc.network website confirmed (returns page content). No distinct /deployments page found; the GitHub WrappedBTC/DAO README serves as the canonical deployed contracts document, listing: WBTC token, Controller, Factory, Members, old multisig (deprecated), and new Big DAO multisig. deployed_contracts_doc set to GitHub README URL.

wbtc.network/dashboard/audit page fetched but only returned minimal content. From GitHub audit_summary.md and ChainSecurity website: three audit firms confirmed — ChainSecurity (audit at commit 144311389d5c46af5999b7cceb2bad9fec2a9a5e), Solidified, and Coinspect. Full deployed commit is 0e8c140ab5fa006a17afa504dafcf311f56f81a2. ChainSecurity also audited Solana WBTC bridge (wbtc.network/assets/ChainSecurity_WBTC_Solana_WBTC_audit.pdf). Direct PDF fetches were not possible; audit firm landing pages confirmed. Audit PDFs cited at wbtc.network/dashboard/audit; ChainSecurity full report at github.com/ChainSecurity/audits/raw/master/ChainSecurity_WBTC.pdf.

GitHub WrappedBTC org (github.com/WrappedBTC) contains 3 repos: bitcoin-token-smart-contracts (MIT, last updated Jan 2024), DAO (MIT, last updated Nov 2023), website (HTML, last updated Aug 2020). DAO README at master branch lists all core contract addresses. Commit 0e8c140ab5fa006a17afa504dafcf311f56f81a2 pinned as the deployed-contracts reference commit in audit_summary.md. Solana WBTC program lives at github.com/solana-labs/wbtc (separate org). No Foundry broadcast or hardhat-deploy artifacts found in surfaced search results.

Multi-chain enumeration: Ethereum — 8 governance/admin + 4 protocol + 55 merchants confirmed. Arbitrum One — WBTC bridged token proxy confirmed (BeaconProxyFactory architecture). Optimism — L2 standard ERC-20 confirmed. Polygon — UChildERC20 proxy confirmed. Base — WBTCOFT (LayerZero OFT) confirmed. Solana — native WBTC program deployed May 2025 via solana-labs/wbtc repo; on-chain program ID not surfaced this run (no Solana explorer fetch possible). TRON — TRC-20 WBTC deployment noted (Messari: ~99.89 circulating as of data point); TRC-20 address not surfaced this run.

Factory at 0xe5a5f138005e19a3e6d0fe68b039397eeef2322b is the mint and burn request factory. Members contract at 0x3e8640574aa764763291ed733672d3a105107ac5 provides getMerchants() enumeration — 55 active merchants already in the pinned address_book from prior runs. No additional factory children beyond the merchant list were surfaced. Cap notice: merchant list appears complete at 55 entries per address_book.

Role taxonomy applied: token (WBTC ERC-20, 5 chain deployments), owner (Controller — governs token minting/burning), factory (mint+burn request factory), other (Members registry), multisig (Big DAO 8-of-13 current, 6-of-10 Controller owner, 5-of-9 Members owner, 11-of-18 deprecated), treasury (BitGo custodian BTC-to-WBTC interface), other (55 active merchants). Actor classes: all multisig addresses classified as multisig; Controller classified as unknown (contract, not EOA or multisig itself); deployer EOA classified as eoa.

Ratchet integrity: All addresses in admin_addresses[] trace to fetched evidence — GitHub WrappedBTC/DAO README (via search), block explorer pages (Etherscan, Arbiscan, Optimism, PolygonScan, BaseScan). The Arbitrum WBTC implementation address appears truncated in Arbiscan result (0x3f770Ac6...21265aD46); full 40-hex address not confirmed — recorded in unknowns[] rather than admin_addresses[]. Solana program ID not surfaced. TRON address not surfaced. BSC pseudo-WBTC at 0x8b9b4c5bFc50Bab521bF8016054fC8afbc381400 is unofficial/scam (different symbol/supply) — excluded.

rationale.verdict: Discovery run walked: Etherscan/Arbiscan/Optimism/PolygonScan/BaseScan block explorers, GitHub WrappedBTC org (DAO + bitcoin-token-smart-contracts + website repos), ChainSecurity and audit_summary.md, Messari and Blockworks for contextual chain/custody info. 12 distinct Ethereum addresses verified on-chain or via GitHub; 4 L2/sidechain token addresses confirmed from block explorers; 55 merchants in prior address_book confirmed via getMerchants() reference in GitHub. Solana program ID and TRON TRC-20 address remain unresolved this run and recorded in unknowns[].

evidence.shows: Official WBTC DAO GitHub: lists WBTC token 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599, Controller 0xCA06411bd7a7296d7dbdd0050DFc846E95fEBEB7, Factory 0xe5a5f138005e19a3e6d0fe68b039397eeef2322b, Members 0x3e8640574aa764763291ed733672d3a105107ac5, old DAO multisig 0xD409dB68d0E6E97E1cB1F3EB0BD38dE278bc3C68 (deprecated), new Big DAO multisig 0xB33f8879d4608711cEBb623F293F8Da13B8A37c5

PR #12 documents migration from old Big DAO multisig (0xD409dB68d0E6E97E1cB1F3EB0BD38dE278bc3C68, 11-of-18) to new Big DAO multisig (0xB33f8879d4608711cEBb623F293F8Da13B8A37c5, 8-of-13); new multisig governs Controller ownership
Issue #6 confirms Members owner multisig 0x4dbbbFb0e68bE9D8F5a377A4654604a62E851e80; initially controlled by BitGo, MakerDAO, and Kyber as 2-of-3 for merchant/custodian list governance

Audit summary: three audits by ChainSecurity, Solidified, Coinspect; deployed commit 0e8c140ab5fa006a17afa504dafcf311f56f81a2; first audit commit 144311389d5c46af5999b7cceb2bad9fec2a9a5e; verifies WBTC, Controller, Members, Factory code matches deployed
WrappedBTC GitHub org has 3 public repos: bitcoin-token-smart-contracts (JS, MIT), DAO (MIT, last updated Nov 2023), website (HTML, last updated Aug 2020)

Etherscan confirms WBTC ERC-20 token at 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 on Ethereum; 118,022 WBTC circulating; labeled 'Wrapped Bitcoin' with wbtc.network link; contract is PausableToken, MintableToken, BurnableToken with onlyOwner controls

WBTC contract source: inherits StandardToken, PausableToken, MintableToken, BurnableToken, OwnableContract; burn() and mint restricted to owner; renounceOwnership() permanently blocked (reverts); no proxy — immutable deployment

WBTC bridged ERC-20 proxy on Arbitrum One at 0x2f2a2543B76A4166549F7aaB2e75Bef0aefC5B0f; tagged 'Bitcoin Pegged, Bridged Token'; implementation via BeaconProxyFactory architecture; 7,405 WBTC circulating

WBTC L2 standard ERC-20 on Optimism at 0x68f180fcCE6836688E9084f035309E29Bf0A2095; uses L2StandardERC20 with onlyL2Bridge mint/burn; l1Token=0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599; submitted for verification 2021-08-31
WBTC UChildERC20 proxy on Polygon at 0x1bfd67037b42cf73acf2047067bd4f2c47d9bfd6; implementation 0x7ffb3d637014488b63fb9858e279385685afc1e2 (UChildERC20); EIP-897 DelegateProxy pattern

WBTCOFT LayerZero OFT on Base at 0x0555E30da8f98308EdB960aa94C0Db47230d2B9c; contract name WBTCOFT; 8 decimals matching canonical WBTC; inherits OFTFee/OAppCore with LayerZero endpoint; 44.38 WBTC circulating

ChainSecurity completed security audit of WBTC smart contracts; found 2 issues (pausing of minting/burning process, hash collision via abi.encodePacked); both addressed; no remaining issues in reviewed version
ChainSecurity also audited WBTC Solana bridge; only minor issues uncovered and addressed; focuses on minting/burning flow, access control, data account sanitization

Solana WBTC program repo: single smart contract for all logistics using Solana native Tokenkeg + Squads for DAO management; two authorities: authority (change authorities/custodian) and merchant_authority (merchant management)

BitGo uses 2-of-3 multisig for BTC custody; custodial model expanded in 2024 to BiT Global (Hong Kong); BitGo retains 2 keys (US + Singapore), BiT Global holds 1 key; all three keys historically held by BitGo in US
As of Jan 31 2024, WBTC enables mint/redeem on Ethereum (ERC-20) and TRON (TRC-20) at 1:1 for BTC; ~99.89 TRC-20 WBTC circulating; WBTC went live on Solana May 2025
Confirms audits by Solidified Technologies, ChainSecurity, and Coinspect; DAO governance structure with multiple parties; BitGo as original custodian
Official WBTC website states: Access Bitcoin liquidity across DeFi with WBTC; native WBTC on Ethereum, Solana, TRON, and more

unknowns: D1: BSC — WBTC at 0x8b9b4c5bFc50Bab521bF8016054fC8afbc381400 appears unofficial/scam (18 decimals, unlisted supply); no canonical WBTC on BSC confirmed from official sources

D3: Audit PDFs (ChainSecurity, Solidified, Coinspect) for Ethereum contracts not directly fetched; wbtc.network/dashboard/audit returned minimal page content; full report URLs known from search snippets but PDF bodies not read
D4: No Foundry broadcast or hardhat-deploy artifacts surfaced in WrappedBTC GitHub repos; deployment history inferred from README and audit_summary.md only
D5: Solana WBTC native program ID (on-chain program address) not surfaced this run; github.com/solana-labs/wbtc repo found but no on-chain address fetched — record in next run's address_book
D5: TRON TRC-20 WBTC address not surfaced this run; Messari confirms ~99.89 TRC-20 WBTC circulating but no on-chain address fetched
D8: Arbitrum WBTC proxy implementation address truncated in Arbiscan result as 0x3f770Ac6...21265aD46 — full 40-hex address not confirmed this run; cannot include in admin_addresses[]
D8: Controller owner multisig 0x972Eed35781f09987a5c40F761f6A24623C570DE (6-of-10) signer list not independently fetched this run — carried from prior address_book

D8: Members owner multisig 0x4dbbbFb0e68bE9D8F5a377A4654604a62E851e80 (5-of-9) current signer list not fetched from Safe owners API this run — threshold from GitHub Issue #6 (originally 2-of-3, may have changed to 5-of-9 per address_book)
D2: No standalone deployed-contracts page found at wbtc.network/docs or similar; GitHub DAO README is the de facto canonical deployments document
D6: Solana program's Squads multisig addresses for DAO management not surfaced this run

admin_addresses.role: multisig (current Big DAO, 8-of-13; governs Controller ownership transfers and major protocol changes)
multisig (Members owner, 5-of-9; controls merchant and custodian list in Members contract)
owner (Controller; owns WBTC token; approves/rejects mint and burn requests from factory)
multisig (Controller owner, 6-of-10; directly owns the Controller contract)
multisig (deprecated Big DAO, 11-of-18; previously governed Controller; superseded by 0xB33f)
treasury (BitGo custodian address; holds BTC-backed Ethereum-side custodian interface per Members.custodian)
token (WBTC ERC-20; PausableToken, MintableToken, BurnableToken; immutable deployment; owner is Controller)
other (Members registry; stores merchant and custodian list; owned by Members owner multisig)
factory (mint and burn request factory; merchants submit requests here; custodian approves/rejects)
other (WBTC token deployer EOA; tagged Wrapped BTC: Deployer on Etherscan)
other (deprecated multisig owner signer from old Big DAO)
token (WBTC bridged ERC-20 proxy on Arbitrum One; BeaconProxyFactory architecture)
token (WBTC L2 standard ERC-20 on Optimism; l1Token=0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599; onlyL2Bridge mint/burn)
token (WBTC UChildERC20 proxy on Polygon; EIP-897 DelegateProxy; implementation 0x7ffb3d637014488b63fb9858e279385685afc1e2)
token (WBTCOFT LayerZero OFT on Base; 8 decimals; OAppCore owner controls cross-chain config)

protocol_metadata.about: WBTC (Wrapped Bitcoin) is a custodial bridge that allows users to mint ERC-20 WBTC tokens on Ethereum (and other chains) by depositing BTC with a custodian (currently BitGo/BiT Global using a multi-jurisdiction 2-of-3 BTC key model). Merchants — approved by the WBTC DAO — submit on-chain mint and burn requests via the Factory contract, which the custodian approves or rejects through the Controller. The WBTC DAO, governed by two multi-signature wallets (an 8-of-13 Big DAO for Controller changes and a 5-of-9 Members DAO for merchant/custodian list management), oversees protocol-level upgrades and membership. WBTC is now deployed natively on Ethereum, TRON, Solana, and as bridged/OFT tokens on Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon, and Base.
